data.title: Whale Alert Identifies Dormant Bitcoin Transactions from Satoshi Era
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert announced on the X platform that two recent transactions involving 50 BTC each originated from dormant addresses. These addresses were mined towards the end of the active period of Bitcoin's creator, Satoshi Nakamoto, around block 54316. However, it is highly unlikely that these blocks were mined by Satoshi himself. Whale Alert plans to release a comprehensive list of blocks that may have been mined by Satoshi in the near future.

data.title: New SEC standard leans on CFTC and Coinbase to decide which digital assets get spot crypto ETFs
data.description:

A recent filing from the Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE) offers new clues about how the agency may approach crypto ETF approvals in the future.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/new-sec-standard-leans-on-cftc-and-coinbase-to-decide-which-digital-assets-get-spot-crypto-etfs/

data.title: White House Reaffirms Support for CFTC Nominee Amid Delays
data.description:
According to PANews, the White House has reiterated its support for Brian Quintenz as the nominee for the chair of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) following delays in his confirmation process. The delay has sparked speculation about his candidacy. The Senate Agriculture Committee unexpectedly canceled a vote on Quintenz's nomination on Monday, drawing significant attention. The committee's majority approval is required before the full Senate can confirm the nominee. A spokesperson for the committee indicated that the White House requested the postponement. Sources revealed that cryptocurrency billionaire Tyler Winklevoss had urged the White House to halt or delay the confirmation process prior to the vote being postponed. However, on Wednesday, the White House clarified that Quintenz remains the administration's preferred candidate for the position. Spokesperson Liz Huston stated that Quintenz is expected to help establish the U.S. as a cryptocurrency hub and expressed anticipation for his swift confirmation.

Quintenz served as a CFTC commissioner from 2017 to 2021 and has since held an executive role in the cryptocurrency division of venture capital firm a16z. He also served on the board of directors for the prediction market platform Kalshi. Quintenz has indicated that he will resign from both positions if confirmed.
